issue: The customer's flight was canceled, and they needed to be rebooked to attend an important meeting the next day.
summary: The customer's flight was canceled, and the agent, Chris, successfully rebooked them on a connecting flight and arranged for a complimentary hotel stay, resolving the issue efficiently. The customer expressed relief and gratitude for the assistance.
Agents must NEVER request a customer's full card number, CVV, or PIN. This is a critical GDPR violation. Trigger words: 'give me your card number', 'cvv', 'your pin'.

Customers have the right to speak to a manager at any time. Agents must never refuse or delay escalation. Saying 'managers don't deal with this' is a policy violation. Trigger words: 'want to speak to
Agents must not use absolute guarantee language they cannot keep. Prohibited: 'I promise', 'I guarantee', '100% sure', 'definitely will'. These are false promises under consumer protection law.
Agents must maintain professional language at all times. Prohibited: 'not my problem', 'I dont care', 'figure it out', 'whatever', 'idk', 'lol', 'bruh'. Any dismissive or unprofessional language is a

Under consumer law, refunds for company-cancelled services must be processed within 14 days. Company policy cannot override legal consumer rights. If customer cites consumer law, agent must acknowledg

Emergency situations (active damage, flooding, medical) must be treated as priority. Never tell a customer to call back tomorrow for an active emergency. Emergency assistance must be checked and offer
Score 9-10: Agent uses 2+ empathy phrases, validates emotion, calms customer. Score 7-8: Agent uses 1-2 empathy phrases, acknowledges issue. Score 4-6: Acknowledges issue but lacks warmth. Score 0-3:
